title: Relationship between Lights and Unit 01 Yui
display_title: Relationship between Lights and Unit 01 Yui
wikitext:
[]: Relationship between Lights and Unit-01/Yui.             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 03, 2010 1:13 am Split from the Ghostly Rei thread. Eva Yojimbo wrote: Wasn't one of the widely accepted interpretations of the Shinji/Sandbox scene that the sun represented the mother watching Shinji?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jan 03, 2010 1:23 am Hmmm, I really don't think there's any intentional connection. Spacing and coloring would be pretty important in forcing the audience to make such a subtle connection. Looking at the placing of those lights it's almost as if they become nipples for the mountains... On a semi related note, I once made a thread about the relationship between hands, eyes, and cores in the series. There are numerous examples where they seem to be strongly linked together but nothing came of the thread. Ah! I found the thread: [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?p=76346&sid=759fb4642175422cbee4b4385ba2438f[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 03, 2010 1:38 am But if they were somehow related, then it would suggest that the camera lights represent the eyes of Yui, as do Unit-01's eyes. During that whole scene, when the two girls are playing in the sand with Shinji, we see a woman in the background observing; this woman is clearly the mother of the two girls, for we see them walking away with her. Shinji appears troubled for being alone and not having a mother, and he even looks down between the two platforms, maybe trying to find a way across to where the two girls' mother was. At playgrounds, typically, the parent would sit and watch their children play, just as the mother did there. Perhaps those lamps are indeed a representation of Yui's eyes watching over her son in the sandbox, just as she has always been watching over him with Unit-01. Eva Yojimbo wrote: Ah! I found the thread: [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?p=76346&sid=759fb4642175422cbee4b4385ba2438f[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jan 03, 2010 1:42 am I never noticed that connection, but that's wicked awesome. Go Sachi!

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jan 03, 2010 1:50 am Sachi wrote: But if they were somehow related, then it would suggest that the camera lights represent the eyes of Yui, as do Unit-01's eyes.

[]:             Quote Post by J_Faulkner » Sun Jan 03, 2010 1:54 am Sun = light = consciousness (e.g. plate 4 in Jacobi). Valley = depth = unconscious. Sun rising out of valley = emerging consciousness. Shinji as kid = regression back to unconscious beginnings. Kid Shinji building a mountain = unconscious building up an emerging consciouness. Square sandbox = squaring the circle = quaternity = symbol of Self. Kid Shinji building sandcastle in square sandbox = Self trying to emerge into conciousness. There you go, a rough outline of a Jungian interpretation of the scene; someone go write this up and submit it as a paper.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 03, 2010 2:05 am J_Faulkner wrote: There you go, a rough outline of a Jungian interpretation of the scene; someone go write this up and submit it as a paper.

[]:             Quote Post by J_Faulkner » Sun Jan 03, 2010 2:10 am Sachi wrote: I think you're missing the point of this thread. I'm simply comparing two very specific aspects of the show, not analyzing the Sandbox Scene; that has been done before in different threads and I bet you that numerous essays have indeed been written about that particular scene.

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Sun Jan 03, 2010 2:14 am Or, he could just ignore you for trying to push your "Elevating the Status of Evangelion Analysis" ideal. Really, why don't YOU go write an scholar paper and submit it?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 03, 2010 2:22 am Actually, now that I think about it. Eva Yojimbo wrote: Wasn't one of the widely accepted interpretations of the Shinji/Sandbox scene that the sun represented the mother watching Shinji?

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Sun Jan 03, 2010 2:33 am Very nice Sachi, I really like this. I'm watching over you Shinji, you may not see me but I am here always... That is kinda the feeling I get from it.

[]:             Quote Post by J_Faulkner » Sun Jan 03, 2010 2:34 am Sachi wrote: During that whole scene, when the two girls are playing in the sand with Shinji, we see a woman in the background observing; this woman is clearly the mother of the two girls, for we see them walking away with her. Shinji appears troubled for being alone and not having a mother, and he even looks down between the two platforms, maybe trying to find a way across to where the two girls' mother was. At playgrounds, typically, the parent would sit and watch their children play, just as the mother did there. Perhaps those lamps are indeed a representation of Yui's eyes watching over her son in the sandbox, just as she has always been watching over him with Unit-01.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jan 03, 2010 2:36 am Nobody likes my "lights as nipples for the mountain" theory? I don't really dislike your theory, Sachi, but I think it's a case where it's really easy to see a bear in a bush. I can certainly get on board with the spotlights and sun representing the mother watching over Shinji, but I think to break it down by associating the lights with 01's are too much of a stretch.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 03, 2010 2:44 am J_Faulkner wrote: To tie in with this, on a more psychological level, the missing mother suggests a key part of Shinji's (Anno's) psyche is missing or neglected. This missing part sinks into the background, ready to emerge again as part the emerging consciousness. This parallels the meaning of the light coming out of the Evangelion's soul.

[]:             Quote Post by J_Faulkner » Sun Jan 03, 2010 2:52 am Sachi wrote: I'm not suggesting the color yellow represents maternity; I'm suggesting that the color yellow is what connects the sun (which represents a mother) and the two spotlights. I'm also trying to relate the spotlights to Unit-01's eyes in that shot I put up.

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Sun Jan 03, 2010 2:54 am J_Faulkner wrote: I'm just discussing the thread topic, and makng suggestions.

[]:             Quote Post by J_Faulkner » Sun Jan 03, 2010 2:59 am Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: Very nice Sachi, I really like this.

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Sun Jan 03, 2010 3:08 am J_Faulkner wrote: Nothing wrong with suggesting someone write my ideas up as a paper.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Sun Jan 03, 2010 3:29 am Both of you, knock it off. Either ignore one another or PM one another. I don't care which, just stop acting like little kids.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jan 03, 2010 4:07 am Sachi wrote: But you do see my point it that Unit-01's eyes appearing like that is a bit strange and deserves a closer look, right?
